title: Multi-system integration case study | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: Multi-system integration case study | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: An anonymised case-study scenario showing how a multi-system integration project can reduce manual work and improve data flow across business operations.

page.title: Multi-system integration case study | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Case study: connecting several systems and reducing manual coordination
page.description: An anonymised case-study scenario showing how a multi-system integration project can reduce manual work and improve data flow across business operations.

hero.title: Data was travelling through people because systems only talked halfway
hero.subtitle: A representative scenario where the company was losing time to re-entry, manual checks, and fragmented information across several systems.

intro: The project started as an integration issue, but it quickly became clear that part of the problem was also process design and how exceptions were handled.
The solution therefore needed both technical integration work and more usable process control around it.

sections.title: Starting situation
body: This is a representative anonymised scenario based on real project patterns, not an inflated marketing story.
bullets: several systems with weak data continuity
reporting dependent on manual data gathering

sections.title: How the work was approached
body: The first step was not a maximalist scope. It was understanding risk, priorities, and the right size of the first delivery phase.
bullets: map source systems and exception paths
prioritise the most valuable integration points
deliver in stages with visible impact
add supporting workflow where pure integration was not enough

sections.title: What changed
body: The point is not to fake exact numbers. It is to show the kind of change a similar project can create.

faq.question: Is technical integration alone enough for this type of project?
faq.answer: Not always. Many projects also need workflow changes and better handling of exceptions.

faq.question: Can this be delivered in stages?
faq.answer: Yes. That is often the most practical way to reduce risk and prove value early.

faq.question: Does it make sense to start with a diagnostic phase?
faq.answer: Yes. Without understanding the data flow and failure impact, it is easy to automate the wrong thing.
